task:
Build this app in Clear:

**Internal Request Queue** (Level 8)

Build an internal request queue for IT/HR/Facilities/Finance tickets. Employees submit a request (title, description, category, priority, submitter). Ops staff can update requests (requires login) — change status, assign to a team, add resolution notes. Need: POST /api/requests (public), GET /api/requests (list), GET /api/requests/new (filter by status='new'), PUT /api/requests/:id (auth required).

Starting skeleton:
```clear
build
final_source:
build for web and javascript backend

database is local memory

owner is 'admin@company.com'
allow signup and login

create a Requests table:
  title, required
  description
  category, default 'IT'
  priority, default 'Normal'
  submitter, required
  status, default 'new'
  assigned_to

# POST /api/requests
when user sends request_data to /api/requests:
  validate request_data:
    title must not be empty
    submitter must not be empty
  saved = save request_data to Requests
  send back saved with status 201

# GET /api/requests
when user calls GET /api/requests:
  requests = get all Requests
  send back requests

# GET /api/requests/new
when user calls GET /api/requests/new:
  requests = get all Requests where status is 'new'
  send back requests

# PUT /api/requests/:id
when user calls PUT /api/requests/:id sending update_data:
  requires login
  updated = save update_data to Requests
  send back updated

page 'Submit Request' at '/':
  section 'New Request':
    title_val = ''
    description_val = ''
    category_val is 'IT'
    priority_val is 'Normal'
    submitter_val = ''
    'Title' is a text input saved as title_val
    'Description' is a text area saved as description_val
    'Category' is a dropdown with ['IT', 'HR', 'Facilities', 'Finance'] saved as category_val
    'Priority' is a dropdown with ['Low', 'Normal', 'High', 'Urgent'] saved as priority_val
    'Your Name' is a text input saved as submitter_val
    button 'Submit':
      send {'title': title_val, 'description': description_val, 'category': category_val, 'priority': priority_val, 'submitter': submitter_val} as a new record to '/api/requests'

page 'All Requests' at '/requests':
  on page load get all_requests from '/api/requests'
  section 'All Requests':
    display all_requests as table

page 'New Tickets' at '/triage':
  on page load get new_requests from '/api/requests/new'
  section 'New Tickets':
    display new_requests as table
